business.type_label: family-friendly coworking + supervised playspace + cafe (physical venue)

engine.ladder_reasoning: Visitor is checking out a physical venue — 36 gallery photos across playspace/cafe/events, hours of operation, map embed, and services describe a place you visit. Browsing the space and its amenities IS the decision.

goal.one_action: book a facility tour (or reserve a pass/membership)

structure.archetype_note: Multi-page local venue site with a membership/pass commerce layer (cart+checkout), tiered pricing tables, a membership builder configurator, and a Calendly tour-booking embed — more than one conversion action, so out of ICP.

mapped.observed_as: Ready to reclaim your focus time?
Drop-In Play / Consistent Care: Monthly Plans
About Us: Our Story & Vision / Our Philosophy
Get in touch / HOURS OF OPERATION

unmapped.why_no_fit: An interactive tool to configure a custom membership plan (select visits/add-ons) that feeds into checkout — not a static pricing or features section

proposed_new.name: membership-checkout
proposed_new.evidence: Squarespace /cart present; tiered monthly membership plans (5/10/15/20 visits + unlimited) with sibling/coworking add-on pricing and a 'Membership Builder' configurator that leads to an on-site purchase — recurring-membership commerce beyond static 'packages' display

confidence.why: Task notes fetch is partial — the Calendly tour-booking form fields sit inside a cross-origin iframe and are not enumerable; commerce/cart flow behind Membership Builder also not fully captured. Structure and engine are clear regardless.

gap_findings: Recurring membership/subscription commerce (tiered plans + add-ons + cart checkout) has no representation in the closed vocab — 'packages' only displays plans, it cannot express on-site purchase/checkout (proposed membership-checkout).
Interactive plan configurator (Membership Builder) — a build-your-own-plan tool distinct from a static pricing table.
Multi-goal venue: tour (Calendly), pass purchase (cart), newsletter (lead-form), contact — the single-one-action model can't cleanly express a venue whose CTAs span booking + commerce + subscribe.
